incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong graph on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the lines on each graph. Which one has the fewest?

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong graph on second attempt

steps.text: Let's count together. Scale of 1 has 20 lines. Scale of 2 has 10 lines. Scale of 5 has 4 lines. Scale of 10 has just 2 lines. Which has the fewest?

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong graph three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Scale of 10 graph highlights to show correct answer.

steps.text: Let me show you. The graph with scale of 10 has only 2 lines to reach 20. That's fewer than any other scale. When each line counts for more, you need fewer lines. Click the scale of 10 graph.
